com.example.myapplication - package com.example.myapplication
 
Comment - Class in com.example.myapplication
This is a comment class it represents one comment It stores the text, date, who made the comment on which device, and when
Comment() - Constructor for class com.example.myapplication.Comment
Empty constructor
Comment(String, String, String, Timestamp) - Constructor for class com.example.myapplication.Comment
Constructor for the Comment class.
CommentActivity - Class in com.example.myapplication
This is the activity which shows the comments for a particular event This displays the comment, the user who wrote the comment, and when they wrote it It also allows for organizers and admins to delete comments
CommentActivity() - Constructor for class com.example.myapplication.CommentActivity
 
CommentAdapter - Class in com.example.myapplication
This is a RecyclerView adapter that allows for a scrollable list of comments This loads the comment information that will be showed for each comment card
CommentAdapter(List<Comment>, String, CommentAdapter.OnCommentLongClickListener, CommentAdapter.OnCommentClickListener, CommentAdapter.OnCommentReactListener) - Constructor for class com.example.myapplication.CommentAdapter
This is the constructor for the adapter, it sets the comment information, the organizer, and a listener for a delete feature
CommentAdapter.OnCommentClickListener - Interface in com.example.myapplication
 
CommentAdapter.OnCommentLongClickListener - Interface in com.example.myapplication
 
CommentAdapter.OnCommentReactListener - Interface in com.example.myapplication
 
CommentTest - Class in com.example.myapplication
This Test class Tests the comments It presses the comment icon in an event and ensures the comment opens It types in the comment box and see it gets posted It tests that the users name exists in the box This Test class was also written with the help of Google "Gemini" LLM Prompt: Given these following scenarios recommend proper test cases to test them - click the comment button on the event, it should open the comments - Open click the comment button on the event, and we should be able to type something in the comment bar - The comment should be posted and that comment should appear in comments - The username of the person who made that comment should be shown
CommentTest() - Constructor for class com.example.myapplication.CommentTest

The following test file was written with the guidance of Claude AI Prompt: "Write UI tests for US 01.09.01" April 2, 2026
